ingredients: 1 1/2 lbs beef top sirloin, fat trimmed and cut into 1" cubes
4 cups watercress leaves or mixed baby greens
instructions: Marinate the 1 1/2 lbs beef: combine 5 crushed garlic cloves, 1 tbsp oyster sauce, 1 tbsp agave (or sugar), 1 tbsp fish sauce, 1 tsp thick soy sauce, and 1 tsp sesame oil with the beef cubes. Refrigerate at least 1 hour, or overnight for best results.
Prepare the vinaigrette: combine 1/2 cup rice vinegar with 1 1/2 tsp kosher salt and 1 1/2 tbsp sugar. It should be a balance of sour, salty, and sweet.
Thinly slice 1 small red onion and toss with about 1/4 cup of the vinaigrette to quick-pickle. Cover and refrigerate for about 10 minutes.
Arrange 4 cups watercress (or mixed baby greens) and 2 thinly sliced tomatoes on a serving platter and set aside.
Make the dipping sauce: squeeze the juice of 1 lime over 1/2 tsp kosher salt and 1/2 tsp fresh cracked black pepper in a small ramekin.

Heat a large wok or pan over high heat. When very hot, add 1/2 tbsp canola oil. When the oil begins to smoke, add half the marinated beef in an even layer. Sear for about 2 minutes undisturbed, then 'shake' the pan to sear opposite sides for 1-2 minutes more to brown all sides.
Remove the first batch and repeat with the remaining 1/2 tbsp canola oil and second batch of beef.
Transfer all beef to the bed of watercress and tomatoes. Drizzle the remaining vinaigrette over the beef and greens, and top with the pickled red onions.
Serve immediately with the lime dipping sauce on the side.
